Our Story

Cooked with love.
Served like family.

Ana Restaurant brings the true flavors of the Dominican Republic to Baltimore's Highlandtown neighborhood on Eastern Ave. Every dish is made fresh, from scratch, with natural seasoning and recipes rooted in tradition.

We started with a simple idea: give this city food that feels like home. Whether you grew up on this food or you've never tried it — you belong at our table.

"Delicious fried fish, chicken, beef, rice, beans, tostones, and salad with a yummy dressing. Generous portions, authentic taste, and the music that transports diners to the Dominican Republic."

★ Google Review Summary · 150 reviews

Menu

What's cooking.

Desayuno — Breakfast
Mangú con Los Tres Golpes
Mashed plantains, fried salami, egg & cheese
Huevos con Salami
Eggs with fried salami
Avena
Traditional Dominican oatmeal
Jugo Natural
Fresh juices — passion fruit, tamarind & more
Almuerzo — Lunch
Pollo Horneado
Rotisserie chicken with rice, beans & salad
Pollo a la Plancha
Grilled chicken breast, well marinated
Chivo Guisado
Slow-cooked goat stew, flavorful and tender
Pescado Frito
Whole fried fish with tostones
Especialidades — Specialties
Mofongo de Camarones
Mashed plantain, shrimp in garlic cream sauce
Fritura Dominicana
Mixed fried platter — chicharrón, salami, yuca
Yaroa
Loaded fries Dominican style
Mondongo
Traditional tripe soup — Sundays only
Acompañamientos — Sides
Tostones
Twice-fried green plantains
Arroz Blanco
White rice
Moro de Habichuelas
Rice cooked with black beans
Ensalada Rusa
Dominican potato salad

También Ofrecemos

Más que
un restaurante.

We also carry a selection of authentic Dominican and Caribbean products — from Rica juices and Choco Rica to Dulcería Piloto sweets and La Lechera. Find the flavors of home right here on Eastern Ave.

Rica Juices Choco Rica Dulcería Piloto La Lechera Dominican Products

What People Are Saying

Baltimore can't stop talking.

★★★★★

"From the moment we stepped in, we felt like family. The staff is extremely friendly. This is true authentic Dominican cooking made with love. The chicharron de pollo was cooked perfectly, the tostones were light and fluffy, and the mofongo was my favorite."

Andrea Ewell Welch
Google · 8 months ago
★★★★★

"10/10 experience! The food was excellent and well seasoned. If you're looking for an authentic Dominican spot, no need to look further. Don't second guess — just come on in and get your taste buds blessed with some good food!"

Xiomara Escobar
Google · 2 weeks ago
★★★★★

"This is my favorite Spanish spot in Baltimore. It reminds me of a place you can find in the Bronx. I try to go at least once a week."

C.W. — Local Guide
Google · 1 month ago · Food 5/5 · Service 5/5
